email.subject: [Test]: MKG_US_ECMPUR_U_SEASONAL_ECOM_EN_03302026_BNew Colors You'll Keep Coming Back To

review.raw_markdown:
## 1. Executive Summary

A clean, color-focused seasonal email built around a single strong idea: new colorways of familiar Skechers styles. The structure is simple and works — one hero, three color panels, a secondary module, then a product grid. The creative execution is visually consistent and the color naming (Slate, Taupe, Olive) does the organizational work well. The main weakness is limited urgency and a footer-heavy bottom half that loses momentum after a strong opening.

---

## 2. Business Impact Score: 6.5 / 10

---

## 3. What's Working

- **Color-as-hero concept lands clearly.** Slate → Taupe → Olive panels are visually distinct, well-photographed, and the naming makes the seasonal palette feel intentional and curated.
- **Visual rhythm is strong.** The repeating color-block + CTA cadence gives the email a confident editorial feel without feeling cluttered.
- **"Cool + Colorful" headline** is punchy and appropriately on-brand for a seasonal refresh.
- **"Shop Our Newest Arrivals" grid** at the bottom efficiently layers in product discovery beyond the featured styles.
- **Mobile layout reads well** — the single-column structure doesn't fight the narrow viewport.

---

## 4. What's Weak

- **No price anchoring anywhere.** A color-launch email to existing purchasers with zero price signal is a missed conversion opportunity — even a "From $XX" would close the gap.
- **CTAs are generic.** "Shop Now" repeated three times across three panels is lazy. "Shop Slate," "Shop Taupe," "Shop Olive" would reinforce the color story and feel less templated.
- **The "More Spring Essentials" mid-section is visually flat.** It breaks the momentum of the color panels without adding much — it reads like a filler module.
- **Subject line is broken.** `[Test]:` prefix is live in the send, and the preview text is raw URL garbage. Neither was cleaned before deployment. This is a deliverability and professionalism issue.
- **No personalization hook visible.** The email is addressed to a segment of existing purchasers but nothing in the body acknowledges purchase history or preferences.

---

## 5. Recommendations

1. **Fix subject line before any real send.** Strip `[Test]:` prefix and write proper preview text — the URL bleed in preview is damaging first impressions at the inbox level.
2. **Differentiate CTAs per color panel** — "Shop Slate," "Shop Taupe," "Shop Olive" — to reinforce the campaign concept and create more specific click intent.
3. **Add a price or value signal** to at least one panel or the arrivals grid. Even "Starting at $65" removes friction.
4. **Cut or sharpen "More Spring Essentials."** If it stays, give it a stronger header and at least 3 visible products with prices. As-is, it's a visual speed bump.
5. **Add an urgency or scarcity note** — "New colors, limited inventory" or a short seasonal window — to drive action beyond browse behavior.

---

## 6. Bottom Line

Solid seasonal concept with good visual discipline, undermined by a broken subject line/preview, repetitive CTAs, and no pricing. For an existing-purchaser segment, this should be converting higher — it's showing the right things to the right people but not asking firmly enough for the sale.

---

## 7. Evidence

**Overall purpose:** Introduce new seasonal colorways (Slate, Taupe, Olive) to existing Skechers purchasers and drive them back to site.

**Hero / primary value proposition:** "Cool + Colorful" headline with a lifestyle/product image. Clear enough, though it doesn't lead with what's new — the color panels below do that work.

**Color panels (Slate / Taupe / Olive):** Three dedicated modules, each with a shoe image on lifestyle background, color name label, and "Shop Now" CTA button. This is the email's strongest section — structured, scannable, and visually coherent.

**"More Spring Essentials" module:** A mid-break section with small product imagery. Feels underdeveloped — products are small, no prices visible, weak header energy.

**"Shop Our Newest Arrivals" product grid:** Horizontal row of ~4 product thumbnails near the bottom. Useful for discovery but no prices or style names are legible at this render size.

**Utility / secondary modules:** Standard footer with nav links, social icons, unsubscribe, and legal text. No rewards/loyalty callout visible despite this being a purchaser segment.

**Bugs / friction:** Subject line contains `[Test]:` prefix — visible to recipients. Preview text is a raw URL string, not marketing copy. No other visible rendering bugs (images load, layout is intact, no broken elements).

---

## Technical Audit

## Technical Audit — MKG_US_ECMPUR_U_SEASONAL_ECOM_EN_03302026_B

---

### 1. Technical Summary

This is a Salesforce Marketing Cloud–sent Skechers seasonal email with a live `[Test]:` subject prefix, systemic HTTP (non-HTTPS) image hosting across all CDN assets, and unverifiable SPF/DKIM authentication status. No broken links or unresolved merge tokens were detected.

---

### 2. Link & Tracking Issues

**Test prefix in subject — production risk**
The subject line was sent as `[Test]: MKG_US_ECMPUR_U_SEASONAL_ECOM_EN_03302026_BNew Colors...`. The `[Test]:` prefix must be stripped before any production deployment. The campaign ID and subject copy also appear directly concatenated (`..._BNew Colors...`), suggesting a missing delimiter in the template configuration.

**Tracking pixels — mixed signals**
Four separate tracking mechanisms are loaded in a hidden table at the top of the email:
- SFMC open pixel: `https://click.emails.skechers.com/open.aspx?N6KPY75DO5WULLEAWA5JCHIFHI...`
- Kruxd user-match beacons (×2): `https://beacon.krxd.net/...` — includes a SHA-256 subscriber identifier (`partner_uid=28374a795c423f502bec51b57d22d407e05bd07626e78a6dea159c79ae67f16a`) and a raw subscriber ID (`partner_uid=2396422`). Passing raw subscriber IDs to third-party pixels warrants a privacy/legal review.
- Return Path seed pixel: `pixel.app.returnpath.net/pixel.gif?r=...` — served over **HTTP** (no scheme specified, defaults to HTTP in most parsers).
- ink1000 tracker: `http://www.ink1000.com/p/up/...` — explicit HTTP.

**26 click-tracked links** all route through `click.emails.skechers.com` — not independently probeable, but redirect chain integrity is unverified.

---

### 3. Rendering & Accessibility

**HTTP image sources — systemic issue**
All images hosted on `image.emails.skechers.com` use `http://` rather than `https://`. Affected assets include the Skechers logo, app store badges, social icons, and footer images. Modern email clients (Gmail, Outlook 2016+, Apple Mail) may block or proxy-strip non-HTTPS images, causing broken rendering.

Affected URLs (representative):
- `http://image.emails.skechers.com/lib/fe3115707564047a731c78/m/1/dde00662-169f-447d-b0e2-fc65f6c2290c.png` (logo)
- `http://image.emails.skechers.com/lib/fe3115707564047a731c78/m/11/0ec56c9f-eec2-471a-898d-0ca1cea7e115.png` (App Store badge)
- `http://image.emails.skechers.com/lib/fe3115707564047a731c78/m/11/9bb508d0-4b38-483d-8459-cf54186a0b9f.png` (Google Play badge)
- Plus 9 additional footer/social images — all HTTP.

**Missing alt text — 4 images**
The following images have empty or absent `alt` attributes, providing no fallback when images are blocked:
- `o.gif` (ink1000 tracker) — minor, but technically non-compliant
- `49468f73-4651-4af3-bea2-61d1ae5db486.png`
- `00100b23-0afa-4715-a8cd-23bb46f9d2b2.png`
- `fc08601a-fcce-4c96-8b90-39e5a55d6c05.png`

The social/app store badge images do have alt text ("Instagram", "Available on the App Store", etc.) — those are fine.

**CSS `display:none` on preheader** is correctly implemented via both class and inline style. Mobile/desktop content switching via `@media screen and (min-width: 768px)` is standard; no issues.

`-webkit-text-size-adjust: none` is present globally — this suppresses iOS automatic font scaling, which may degrade readability at small viewport widths. Low severity.

---

### 4. Personalization & Merge Tokens

No unresolved merge tokens (e.g., `%%FIRST_NAME%%`, `{{email}}`) detected in the visible HTML. The preheader text is hardcoded: `"Your Favorite Colors are BOGO 50% Off"` — no dynamic substitution.

The Kruxd beacon passes `partner_uid=2396422` as a plain subscriber ID. If this is an internal SFMC subscriber key or PII-derived value, confirm it is intentional and compliant with your DMP data-sharing agreement.

---

### 5. Compliance

**SPF/DKIM — unverifiable**
QA check reports: `Authentication-Results header not found`. SPF and DKIM pass/fail status cannot be confirmed from the relay path through AgentMail. This must be verified directly against the sending domain `emails.skechers.com` via DNS (`dig TXT _domainkey.emails.skechers.com`) and a live header inspection.

**CAN-SPAM**
- Physical mailing address: present (assumed in footer — not visible in truncated source, but standard footer structure is present).
- Unsubscribe mechanism: present via SFMC standard footer link pattern.
- From/Reply-To identity (`no-reply@emails.skechers.com`): valid sender identity disclosed.

No CAN-SPAM violations confirmed from available source, but full footer must be present in production send.

---

### 6. Email-to-Site Continuity

All CTA links route through `click.emails.skechers.com` click-tracking redirects. The final destination URLs and their UTM parameter payloads are not inspectable without resolving the redirect chain (skipped by QA probe). UTM attribution (`utm_source`, `utm_medium`, `utm_campaign`) should be verified to confirm `utm_campaign=MKG_US_ECMPUR_U_SEASONAL_ECOM_EN_03302026_B` is appended on all destination URLs.

The campaign ID in the Kruxd impression beacon (`campaignid=MKG_US_ECMPUR_U_SEASONAL_ECOM_EN_03302026_B`) matches the email campaign name — consistent.

---

### 7. Recommendations

| Priority | Issue | Action |
|---|---|---|
| **Critical** | `[Test]:` prefix in subject line | Remove before production deployment |
| **High** | All `image.emails.skechers.com` assets served over HTTP | Update CDN image URLs to `https://` |
| **High** | ink1000 and Return Path pixels over HTTP | Update to `https://` or confirm CDN forces HTTPS upgrade |
| **High** | SPF/DKIM status unverified | Run live header inspection on production send; confirm `emails.skechers.com` DKIM record is published |
| **Medium** | 3 content images missing `alt` text | Add descriptive alt text (or `alt=""` for decorative images) to `49468f73`, `00100b23`, `fc08601a` PNGs |
| **Medium** | Raw subscriber ID passed to Kruxd (`partner_uid=2396422`) | Confirm this is hashed/anonymized per DMP data agreement |
| **Low** | UTM params on destination URLs unverified | Manually resolve one or two click links to confirm UTM chain is intact |
| **Low** | Campaign ID concatenated into subject (`_BNew Colors`) | Verify template subject field delimiter is correct in SFMC |
